title: Today's Tech Brief: Google's AI Push and Data Center Drama
summary: Google's AI enshittification, data center secrecy, and tech updates
imageAlt: Image from source article for Google has seriously leaned into AI enshittification lately

imageSourceTitle: Google has seriously leaned into AI enshittification lately

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your tech brief. I've got a few stories to share today that caught my eye in the world of tech. Google's been making some big moves with AI, and not everyone is thrilled. Let's dive in. I've been following this stuff for a while now, and I've got to say, it's getting interesting.

First up, Google's been leaning into AI enshittification. For those who might not know, enshittification refers to the decline in quality of a product or service as it becomes more commercialized. In this case, Google's AI push has raised concerns that the web might become less useful. I mean, who wants to sift through pages of AI-generated content just to find what they're looking for? It's like trying to find a needle in a haystack, but the haystack is made of robotic writing.

Also making waves is Erin Brockovich's take on data center secrecy. She's taking aim at companies that keep their data center operations on the down-low. It's an interesting move, and I'm curious to see how it plays out. Data centers are the backbone of our digital world, after all. We rely on them to store our data, but do we really know what's going on behind the scenes?

Google has seriously leaned into AI enshittification lately is also worth a quick mention. My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.
